page: Amazon Web Services r8g.24xlarge is a memory-optimized server instance powered by custom AWS Graviton Neoverse-V2 processors. Built on the arm64 architecture, this instance features 96 dedicated physical cores running at 2.8 GHz with a single thread per core. It is equipped with 768.0 GB of system memory, providing a memory-to-core ratio of 8.0 GB per core. The processor includes 64 KB of L1 data cache, 2 MB of L2 cache, and 36 MB of L3 cache. It operates on the AWS Nitro hypervisor. There is no local NVMe storage bundled with this instance, and it does not include any hardware GPU accelerators. Network connectivity is delivered via a 40 Gbps baseline bandwidth.

Benchmark data highlights top-tier performance in memory-intensive and multi-threaded workloads. The instance achieves top 10% placement in stress-ng multi-core CPU performance, Geekbench multi-core scores, and PassMark memory tests. Memory bandwidth is a primary strength, with read-only small block cached bandwidth and large block uncached read/write bandwidth all ranking in the top 10%. Database operations and Redis SET operations also perform in the top tier, processing up to 195.4 million operations per second. However, single-core performance is more moderate, with stress-ng single-core CPU performance falling into the middle 50% average category.

From a cost-efficiency perspective, the r8g.24xlarge offers high resource density and memory bandwidth per unit of compute, making it highly efficient for large-scale in-memory databases, caching layers, and memory-bound enterprise applications. The lack of local storage requires reliance on network-attached storage, which is a key architectural tradeoff to consider for disk-heavy workloads. This instance is suited for memory-intensive workloads such as Redis deployments, large-scale database operations, static web serving, and medium-to-large LLM inference text generation where it demonstrates top-tier execution speeds.

It features 768.0 GB of RAM, yielding a high memory-to-core ratio of 8.0 GB per core, but includes no local storage or GPU accelerators. Operating on the Nitro hypervisor, the instance delivers a 40 Gbps baseline network bandwidth. Benchmark results place this server in the top 10% for multi-core CPU performance, memory bandwidth, and database operations, though single-core performance remains in the middle tier. This hardware profile is highly efficient for memory-intensive applications, offering high resource density for in-memory databases and caching layers. It is suited for workloads such as Redis deployments, large-scale database operations, static web serving, and LLM inference.
